sanskrit:
सहयज्ञाः प्रजाः सृष्ट्वा पुरोवाच प्रजापतिः।
अनेन प्रसविष्यध्वमेष वोऽस्त्विष्टकामधुक्॥
transliteration: saha-yajñāḥ prajāḥ sṛṣṭvā purovāca prajāpatiḥ | anena prasaviṣyadhvam eṣa vo ’stiṣṭa-kāma-dhuk ||
literal_meaning: Having created the beings together with sacrifice, the Lord of creatures of old declared: 'By this (sacrifice) may you bring forth; may it be your wish-fulfilling cow.'
contextual_meaning: The psyche is hard-wired for cooperative action: when we align personal effort (yajña) with a purpose larger than the ego, life becomes generative and desire is sustainably fulfilled.
psychological_state: Healthy striving—motivation rooted in shared abundance rather than scarcity.
trigger_scenario: A team member who usually hoards credit begins sharing resources because the leader frames the project as a communal ritual where everyone’s contribution feeds back prosperity.
anchor_text: Work for the common good and your needs will naturally be met.
